summary: Templates feature complete testing - Backend 19/19 tests passed (100%), Frontend UI fully functional. Fixed use_count bug in backend that wasn't returning updated count after template use.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/templates - Supervisor can create template (200)
Backend: POST /api/templates - Televendas can create template (200)
Backend: POST /api/templates - Validation rejects missing required fields (422)
Backend: GET /api/templates - List all templates for user
Backend: GET /api/templates?search= - Search templates by name/client name
Backend: GET /api/templates?tag= - Filter templates by tag
Backend: GET /api/templates/{id} - Get single template
Backend: GET /api/templates/{id} - Returns 404 for non-existent template
Backend: PUT /api/templates/{id} - Update template
Backend: PUT /api/templates/{id} - Returns 404 for non-existent template
Backend: DELETE /api/templates/{id} - Delete template and verify removal
Backend: DELETE /api/templates/{id} - Returns 404 for non-existent template
Backend: POST /api/templates/{id}/use - Use template returns auto-fill data
Backend: POST /api/templates/{id}/use - Use count increments correctly
Backend: POST /api/templates/{id}/use - Suggests date based on preferred_day_of_week
Backend: POST /api/templates/{id}/use - Returns 404 for non-existent template
Backend: Templates are scoped to creator only (user isolation)
Backend: Templates sorted by use_count (most used first)
Backend: Date suggestion from last appointment (+7 days)
Frontend: 'Usar Template' button visible in appointment modal
Frontend: Template selector opens with search input
Frontend: Template cards show name, client, time, day, tags, use_count
Frontend: 'Salvar como Template' button appears when name/sobrenome filled
Frontend: Template form modal pre-fills with appointment data
Frontend: Template auto-fill populates Nome, Sobrenome, Chat, Date correctly
Frontend: Toast message confirms template application
Frontend: Template creation through UI works
Frontend: Template search returns correct results
Frontend: Televendas user can access template features
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Fixed use_template endpoint to return updated use_count after increment
/app/backend/tests/test_templates_feature.py - Created comprehensive template feature test suite
seed_data_creation: Templates created during testing: 'João Silva - Semanal' (pre-existing), 'TEST_AutomacaoTemplate' (created via UI test). Test templates with 'TEST_' prefix are cleaned up by pytest fixtures.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Templates feature fully implemented and tested. Endpoints: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/templates, POST /api/templates/{id}/use. UI components: TemplateSelector (data-testid=template-selector), TemplateFormModal (data-testid=template-form-modal), use-template-btn, save-template-btn in AppointmentModal. Templates are user-scoped (created_by field). Use endpoint returns auto-fill suggestion with suggested_date (+7 days from last appointment or based on preferred_day_of_week). Fixed bug where use_count wasn't being returned as updated value after increment.

template_use.returns: Template data + suggestion (first_name, last_name, has_chat, notes, suggested_date, suggested_time_slot, last_appointment)
template_use.date_calculation: If client has previous appointments: +7 days from last visit. If preferred_day_of_week set: next occurrence. Default: tomorrow
template_use.use_count: Incremented on each use, returned in response

template_features.has_chat: Boolean, pre-fills chat toggle when template used
template_features.use_count: Counter incremented on each use, sorted by this field

bug_fixed.issue: use_template endpoint returned old use_count (0) instead of updated value (1, 2, etc.) after incrementing
bug_fixed.root_cause: Template was fetched before the $inc update and returned without re-fetching
bug_fixed.fix: Added re-fetch of template after update: `updated_template = await db.appointment_templates.find_one({'id': template_id}, {'_id': 0})` and returned `AppointmentTemplate(**updated_template)` instead of original template
